German ruling declares Google liable for false answers in AI Overviews
AI agent runs amok in Fedora and elsewhere
If Claude Fable stops helping you, you'll never know
CEOs who think AI replaces their employees are just bad CEOs
Anthropic requires 30 day data retention for Fable and Mythos
Claude Desktop spawns 1.8 GB Hyper-V VM on every launch, even for chat-only use
Apple reveals new AI architecture built around Google Gemini models

techcrunch.title: Pool’s new app turns your screenshots into something useful
techcrunch.summary: Pool's new app automatically sorts screenshots into personalized collections, tracks down the original links behind saved content, and helps you rediscover products, recipes, travel ideas, and other things you meant to revisit.

techcrunch.title: DoorDash’s new AI chatbot lets you order with prompts and photos
techcrunch.summary: The new chatbot, called Ask DoorDash, allows users to search the app for what they're looking for in their own words instead of having to scroll through restaurants and stores to build a cart.

techcrunch.title: Opendoor’s India exit is fueling a bigger conversation about AI and outsourcing
techcrunch.summary: The decision comes as India emerges as the world’s largest GCC market.

techcrunch.title: Anthropic’s Dario Amodei has just one direct report
techcrunch.summary: If you doubted his genius, doubt no more.

techcrunch.title: xAI fired an engineer who raised alarms about Grok safety, new lawsuit claims
techcrunch.summary: A former xAI engineer is suing the company and SpaceX, alleging he was fired for raising AI safety concerns about Grok days before SpaceX's historic IPO.

techcrunch.title: Fresh off bond sale, Amazon borrows $17.5B from banks as AI spending continues
techcrunch.summary: Companies are burning through exorbitant sums of money to keep pace in the AI arms race. Debt is climbing.

techcrunch.title: ‘AI-pilled’ firms spend $7,500 per employee each month on AI
techcrunch.summary: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.

techcrunch.title: How memory tools can make AI models worse
techcrunch.summary: New research suggests that AI memory systems can degrade model performance and encourage sycophantic tendencies.

techcrunch.title: Cybersecurity researchers aren’t happy about the guardrails on Anthropic’s Fable
techcrunch.summary: Cybersecurity researchers are complaining that Anthropic's new model Fable has guardrails that are too strict for any cybersecurity work.

techcrunch.title: Datadog veterans launch AI coding startup Niteshift on a bet against Big AI lock-in
techcrunch.summary: AI coding agent startup Niteshift has raised a $7 million seed round from a who's who of angels. It's betting companies will want power over, not lock-in with model makers.
